headline: Advances in Quantum Hardware, Algorithms, and Materials
summary: Today's research highlights key developments across the quantum stack, from hardware-level optomechanical sensors and silicon defect modeling to algorithmic improvements in quantum annealing. Additionally, researchers have made strides in formalizing cryptographic attacks and designing high-rate quantum error-correcting codes.

themes.title: Quantum Hardware & Physical Systems
themes.body: Developments in physical quantum systems include benchmarking trigonometric continuous-variable gates on trapped ions, modeling G-center defects in silicon for spin-optical applications, and designing high-Q sail membranes for optomechanical accelerometers.

themes.title: Algorithms, Proofs, and Error Correction
themes.body: Theoretical and algorithmic breakthroughs feature agentic AI formalization of Shor's algorithm in Lean, new pair-partition constructions for high-rate quantum LDPC codes, and ZZ-catalysts that reshape quantum annealing landscapes to avoid local minima.

connections.body: Both papers address the practical scaling limits of quantum computing, with one formalizing the exact resource requirements for Shor's algorithm and the other constructing high-rate quantum LDPC codes to protect those physical qubits.
